tools.name: snapshot
tools.description: Show current state from the runtime registry for one sandbox, or aggregate ready manager-known sandboxes when --sandbox-id is omitted: sandbox lifecycle state, workspaces (with layer counts), in-flight executions, and the latest resource sample per scope. Served live; does not read the log.

sandbox_id.description: Optional target sandbox id. When omitted, the manager queries all ready sandboxes.

tools.name: trace
tools.description: Fold the log into a span waterfall for one trace: spans nested by parent, offset by start, with attached events inline. Use --trace-id last for the most recent root trace.

sandbox_id.description: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

trace_id.description: Trace id to render, or 'last' for the most recent root trace.

tools.name: events
tools.description: Fold the log into a flat, cross-trace stream of point-in-time events (lease, errors, …), newest first. Filter by exact name and/or a start timestamp, and cap to the newest N with --last-n.

sandbox_id.description: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

name.description: Filter to events with this exact name (e.g. lease.acquired).

since_ms.description: Only events at or after this unix-ms timestamp.

last_n.description: Keep only the N newest matched events.

tools.name: cgroup
tools.description: Fold the sample log for one scope into a time series with deltas: cgroup counters (cpu/mem/io from /sys/fs/cgroup) plus the disk sample (upperdir bytes/files) carried in the same record.

sandbox_id.description: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

scope.description: Resource scope: 'sandbox' or a workspace id.

tools.name: layerstack
tools.description: Show the active manifest as a per-layer inventory: disk bytes, how many workspaces lease each layer, and which leased layers book each base. Served live from the runtime; does not read the log.

sandbox_id.description: Target sandbox id (selects the daemon to query).

workspace_id.description: Show one workspace's lower layers and private upperdir.

window_ms.description: Lookback window in milliseconds for the stack trend (max 600000).
